title: Pregnant and Postpartum Women Treatment Reauthorization Act
short_title: Pregnant and Postpartum Women Treatment Reauthorization Act
plain_summary: This bill renames a law that helps pregnant women and new mothers who have problems with drugs or alcohol get the treatment they need. The government will spend more money on this program, increasing it from about thirty million dollars to about thirty nine million dollars each year. This extra funding will continue for the next five years to help more women get better. The bill also makes some small improvements to how the program works to help families.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Pregnant and Postpartum Women Treatment Reauthorization Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Reauthorization of Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called Section 508 of the Public Health Service Act. It makes three changes. First, it replaces the words health services with health care services in one part of the law. Second, it requires applicants to provide a plan that describes their services. The plan can also explain how they will reach out to women who are especially affected by drug and alcohol problems during and after pregnancy. Third, it increases the money available for this program from twenty nine million nine hundred thirty one thousand dollars per year for the years two thousand nineteen through two thousand twenty three to thirty eight million nine hundred thirty one thousand dollars per year for the years two thousand twenty five through two thousand twenty nine.
